intro: Vulpia dertonensis, also known as Derton Fescue, is a species of grass within the genus Vulpia, characterized by its delicate stems and small, flower-like inflorescences. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Vulpia dertonensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vulpia dertonensis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function, soothe respiratory issues, and promote overall well-being.
